name: QTB: Tighten & Finalise
description: Revises the QTB incorporating verification findings and scrutiny test feedback. Tightens language and checks format.
prompt_template:
<task>
We've now worked through the QTB draft, the claim verification table, and the parliamentary scrutiny test in this conversation. Please revise and finalise the brief.

Instructions:
1. Address the Top 3 issues from the scrutiny test
2. Remove or replace any HIGH RISK / POTENTIALLY FABRICATED claims from the verification table – use [VERIFY: description] where I still need to check something
3. Do not invent replacement data – use [INSERT DATA] where verified data is needed
4. Tighten the Key Messages – they should be punchy, quotable, and under 25 words each
5. Check talking points are direct and avoid evasive language
6. Keep within {{length}}

After the revised QTB, provide a short CHANGE SUMMARY:
- What was changed and why
- Any [VERIFY] or [INSERT DATA] items still needing action
- Any scrutiny feedback you didn't address and why
</task>

variables.name: length
variables.description: Target length of the final QTB

beginner.notes: Before running this step, resolve as many [VERIFY] items as you can – replace with real figures or remove the claims. Don't submit a brief with unverified statistics.

intermediate.notes: Read the Key Messages aloud – if they don't sound natural for the reader to say, rewrite them yourself. The model writes plausible language, not the reader's actual voice.

advanced.notes: Run QG-TRUST-001 on the revised draft to catch any errors introduced in revision. Brief finalisation is when new errors are most likely to creep in.

known_limitations: Revision may introduce new errors – always do a final human read before the brief is used.
The model cannot verify claims – all [VERIFY] items require human action before submission.
